title: Instrumentality Safeguard by FAR
wikitext:
[]: Instrumentality = Safeguard by FAR?             Quote Post by jenova7 » Thu Dec 13, 2007 7:28 am just something i wanted to know: could instrumentality have been intentionally created by the first ancestral race as a safeguard against adam and lilith eggs colliding on the same planet?

[]:             Quote Post by V » Thu Dec 13, 2007 8:48 am no it wasn't a safeguard. Indeed, they had multiple safeguards in place (the Lances, the Dead Sea Scrolls) to try to PREVENT Instrumentality, because it would create a god-like being on par with themselves.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Thu Dec 13, 2007 9:14 am I think the idea that they were afraid of creating a God-like being on par with themselves is fanwanking things a bit too far. In reality, we know next to nil about the FAR. What we do know has come from the CI and a mostly speculation based on what happened in the series.

[]:             Quote Post by Shiro » Thu Dec 13, 2007 11:06 am Because if they were trying to prevent Instrumentality, then why would they create a SoL with that capability? You'd think that incredibly powerful beings like the F.A.R. would have the foresight to design their creations without the ability to create a godlike being, if they didn't want that. A civilization that could create a SoL would have been intelligent enough to see that possibility, if they were against it.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Thu Dec 13, 2007 11:10 am Really... Just create all ADO races or all LDO races; problem solved.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Thu Dec 13, 2007 11:18 am I'm not sure I understand the question.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Thu Dec 13, 2007 12:26 pm Jimbo wrote: I think the idea that they were afraid of creating a God-like being on par with themselves is fanwanking things a bit too far. In reality, we know next to nil about the FAR. What we do know has come from the CI and a mostly speculation based on what happened in the series.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Thu Dec 13, 2007 12:43 pm Shin-seiki wrote: Where does 'fanwanking' (I loath that word, btw) come in here? It's all readily defensible conclusions.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Thu Dec 13, 2007 12:58 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: You're trying to determine the motives of God-beings on a higher plane of existence than us

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Thu Dec 13, 2007 1:11 pm BobBQ wrote: And how, exactly, do we know that?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Dec 13, 2007 1:15 pm I think BobBQ meant "How do we know that the FAR exist on a higher plane of existence than us?"

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Dec 13, 2007 1:22 pm If God didn't want Adam and Eve to eat from the Tree of Knowledge, why'd he put it right in the Garden of Eden where they could pluck a fruit at any time? ...is this the sort of question we're dealing with?

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Thu Dec 13, 2007 1:45 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: Can you make Gods without being Gods yourself?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Dec 13, 2007 1:58 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: Can you make Gods without being Gods yourself?

[]:             Quote Post by Shiro » Thu Dec 13, 2007 7:05 pm Man can create something that Man can no longer control, and is far more powerful than anything Man possesses. From Man's perspective, they created a God.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Thu Dec 13, 2007 7:08 pm "God creates dinosaurs, god destroys dinosaurs, god creates man, man destroys god, man creates dinosaurs..." "...Dinosaurs eat man, woman inherits the earth."

[]:             Quote Post by Shiro » Thu Dec 13, 2007 7:11 pm And then with nothing to mate with, woman dies too. Dinosaurs rule the world.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Dec 13, 2007 7:28 pm Shiro wrote: Man can create something that Man can no longer control

[]:             Quote Post by Shiro » Thu Dec 13, 2007 7:38 pm Yeah, but what happens when the Spears are lost? What can Man do then? One of em was gone for good, the other got lost in space for a while. That shows you how little Man can do against them. And it's not like the SoL are killed, just in a sort of coma until the LnY is removed.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Thu Dec 13, 2007 7:50 pm Leaving out the 2001 influence (Where the firstborn supposedly went past all physical form), there really is nothing in the series to show what happened to the FAR, or whether they left to a higher plane of existence. For all you know, they might be lurking around the galaxy waiting for their creations to mature and make contact with them. And they might be very, very Lilin-like. Lilin, as opposed to ADO life, represent evolution in its natural form. ADO are much more created than evolved, since they get to choose their own form over their original humanoid form (Sandalphon is very explicit). While Lilin-type intelligent life could concievably have evolved without any assistance from an SoL, Lilith's inclusion might have ensured that the finished product had human form. The seawater of primitive earth might resemble the LCL-Shinji mix because Lilith added some of her own compounds to the mix to divert the evolutionary process subtly at the beginning. Adam-derived life, no matter how dumb, are deadly in their nature to evolve. Sachi grew smarter (and much deadlier) after the N2 attack. Arael makes an actual attempt to probe another mind, and Armisael takes Angel intelligence to the highest level. Even Ireul came within a second of defeating the Magi. Having Armisael come in mere months after Sachiel first showed up suggests that ADO can evolve their abilities extremely quickly when needed, so an ADO could probably evolve to near-God or God status if needed. Creating ADOs is a stopgap method of creation, and a potentially dangerous one to a non-FAR race. As a matter of fact, I thought about it as Angels being ones to assuage FAR loneliness, while Lilin are reassurance for the distant future- when even the FAR might not survive, many Lilin species will exist to learn from their creations and start creation anew. Instrumentality might not have been planned, but an unfortunate consequence of the method of creation involved. The original FAR, souls or not, would probably be immune to human or Angel instrumentality.
summary:
[]: Instrumentality = Safeguard by FAR?             Quote Post by jenova7 » Thu Dec 13, 2007 7:28 am just something i wanted to know: could instrumentality have been intentionally created by the first ancestral race as a safeguard against adam and lilith eggs colliding on the same planet?

[]:             Quote Post by V » Thu Dec 13, 2007 8:48 am no it wasn't a safeguard. Indeed,
